steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two ideas
steps.content: Sentence 1 establishes a general rule: ABA triggers a slowdown in most plants. The next sentence introduces an exception: Schrenkiella parvula produces ABA yet shows accelerated growth. Slowdown versus acceleration is a direct opposition — the transition must signal contrast.

steps.title: Match each transition to that relationship
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Moreover,': Signals addition — used to extend or reinforce the previous idea, not contradict it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In contrast,': Signals opposition — correctly introduces a result that is the opposite of what was established. ✓
  • Choice C — 'For example,': Signals illustration — would mean Schrenkiella parvula is an example of the slowdown rule, but it is actually an exception. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Thus,': Signals a logical consequence — would imply the accelerated growth follows from the slowdown rule, which reverses the logic. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
